articles.title: Trump administration vows crackdown on Chinese firms 'exploiting' U.S. AI models
articles.summary: The Trump administration is vowing to crack down on foreign tech companies' exploitation of U.S. artificial intelligence models, singling out China at a time that country is narrowing the gap with the

articles.title: War and Sanctions Accelerate China’s Currency Push
articles.summary: China’s bid to build a renminbi-based financial system beyond the U.S. dollar’s reach is gaining traction as a way to sidestep sanctions.

articles.title: Iran War Has Drained U.S. Supplies of Critical, Costly Weapons
articles.summary: The Pentagon’s rush to rearm its Mideast forces makes it less ready to confront potential adversaries like Russia and China, administration and congressional officials say.

articles.title: Trump Reposts Anti-Immigrant Tirade Calling China and India ‘Hellhole’ Places
articles.summary: The president touched off a furor with his post sharing a podcast episode in which the host referred to China and India as “hellhole” places.

articles.title: 2 young people arrested in alleged plot to attack Houston synagogue
articles.summary: Two young people have been arrested in an alleged plot to attack a Texas synagogue that involved driving through the congregation to "kill as many Jews as possible," according authorities.

articles.title: EU approves a $106 billion loan package to help Ukraine after Hungary lifts its veto
articles.summary: The European Union on Thursday approved a $106-billion loan package to help Ukraine meet its economic and military needs for two years, ending months of political deadlock.

articles.title: Concern Grows Over Republican Congressman’s Mysterious Absence
articles.summary: A spokesman for Representative Thomas Kean Jr. said an unspecified “personal medical issue” has led the New Jersey Republican to miss House votes.

articles.title: Pentagon Fires Stars and Stripes Newspaper’s Ombudsman
articles.summary: The newspaper’s ombudsman, Jacqueline Smith, said she had been given no reason for her dismissal.

articles.title: Soldier Used Classified Information to Bet on Maduro’s Ouster, U.S. Says
articles.summary: Federal prosecutors say that Sgt. Gannon Ken Van Dyke, who was involved in the operation to oust Nicolás Maduro from power in Venezuela, used the information to place bets on a prediction market.

articles.title: U.S. soldier charged with using classified information to bet on Maduro's removal
articles.summary: It's the first time suspected insider trading on Polymarket has led to criminal charges in the U.S.

articles.title: French police probe suspected weather device tampering after odd Polymarket bet
articles.summary: The incident is the latest eyebrow-raising bet on Polymarket, as allegations of rigging and manipulation continue to haunt the popular prediction market site.

articles.title: How the Iran War Is Morphing Into a Volatile Standoff in the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The conflict has morphed into a volatile standoff in the Strait of Hormuz, as the economic costs mount and President Trump faces a political backlash at home.

articles.title: Trump’s Dreams for a Battleship Led to His Navy Secretary’s Ouster
articles.summary: The Navy secretary, John Phelan, was supposed to deliver the first of the president’s ships by 2028. The timeline was nearly impossible.

articles.title: The FDA gives the green light to the first gene therapy for deafness
articles.summary: The treatment, developed by Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, is for a very rare form of deafness. But it represents a medical milestone.

articles.title: Meta will lay off 10% of its staff
articles.summary: The cuts follow losses in two pivotal court cases and the company's push to invest in artificial intelligence.

articles.title: Airlines in Europe slash thousands of flights as Iran war cuts jet fuel supplies
articles.summary: The soaring cost of jet fuel is forcing European airlines to cancel tens of thousands of flights, while energy authorities warned of a possible jet fuel shortage if supplies aren't replenished soon.

articles.title: Tensions rise in two ceasefires in the Middle East. And, the Navy secretary ousted
articles.summary: Tensions are rising in the Middle East as shaky ceasefire agreements between the U.S., Israel and Iran, and Lebanon and Israel, are tested. And, the Secretary of the Navy is out of the role.

articles.title: Trump announces a 3-week extension of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
articles.summary: President Trump said Thursday that Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend their ceasefire for three weeks after the two sides met at the White House for a high-level negotiation.

articles.title: Peace talks between U.S. and Iran at a standstill as Trump extends ceasefire
articles.summary: President Trump has extended the ceasefire, but Iran says it's not enough if the naval blockade is still in place.

articles.title: How TikTok is driving American expats to Southeast Asia
articles.summary: Americans who moved to Vietnam and Thailand say their lives are now lower-stress and lower-cost. But glamorous videos on TikTok don't tell the whole story.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
